title: Parse a length-prefixed payload using binary size modifier
prompt: Given: `def decode(<<len::unsigned-integer-size(16), rest::binary>>) do payload = :binary.part(rest, 0, len); trailer = :binary.part(rest, len, byte_size(rest) - len); {payload, trailer} end`. Rewrite the single function head to destructure the payload and trailer directly: `def decode(<<len::unsigned-integer-size(16), payload::binary-size(len), trailer::binary>>)` returning `{payload, trailer}`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Framer do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Runtime-variable binary size is a less-common feature. Needs `binary-size(len)` in a pattern position.
